author: sukunrt
content: is there a doc for scoring? the specs dont have much detail here.

author: aarshshahoffchain
content: <@477396362917576713> You can just look at go-libp2p-gossipsub scoring for partial messages to start with. It's very binary (good message/bad message) and gives as much credit for a  partial message (no matter how small it is) as it does for a full message (with the added pressure of full message peers having to be first/near-first for a message from time to time where as partial message peers have no such presurre/penalty).

author: aarshshahoffchain
content: We can discuss in more detail in tomorow's WG call and I can put together a doc after that that includes our discussions tomorrow.

author: aarshshahoffchain
content:
<@106441459183423488> Okay this looks good now post deployment. Thanks !

The impact is more pronounced for non-super nodes  because even earlier, super nodes would subscribe to all data column topics with the partial extension enabled and so when they did  do a broadcast as a proposer, the broadcast would go via the partial extension. That is not the case for non-super nodes. They join a topic lazily during the broadcast (and because of the bug, the partial extension was disabled during this lazyy join).
